# Team News: Saudi Arabia vs. Uruguay injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Matt Law| 14 Jun 2026 23:00, Last updated: 14 Jun 2026 23:00

© Iconsport / DeFodi Images

Saudi Arabia and Uruguay will begin their respective 2026 World Cup campaigns with a contest in Miami on Monday.

The two nations are competing in Group H this summer alongside Spain and Cape Verde, and here Sports Mole rounds up the team news for the game.

---

## SAUDI ARABIA VS. URUGUAY

## SAUDI ARABIA

Out: Nawaf Al Aqidi (muscle)

Doubtful:

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Al-Owais; Abdulhamid, Al Tambakti, Lajami, Bu; Kanno, Al-Khaibari; N Al-Dawsari, Al-Juwayr, S Al-Dawsari; Al-Buraikan

## URUGUAY

Out: None

Doubtful: Jose Gimenez(ankle), Ronald Araujo(calf), Giorgian de Arrascaeta (calf), Matias Vina(muscle), Sebastian Caceres (head)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Muslera; Varela, Ca

# Saudi Arabia vs. Uruguay projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group H game in Miami Gardens

Joe Wright

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Saudi Arabia face Uruguay on Monday as Group H play at the 2026 World Cup begins.

Spain take on Cape Verde in the earlier game before Marcelo Bielsa's team take on a Saudi side they met at this stage of the competition back in 2018.

The European champions are expected to finish top of this pool, but Uruguay will believe they can challenge La Roja and will be eager to lay down an early marker. For Georgios Donis and his players, this game is all about finding a way to get a result that will give them at least some hope of progressing to the Round of 32.

Below is the latest team news and projected lineups for both sides.

2026 WORLD CUP HQ: Latest World Cup news| Full World Cup schedule| Buy World Cup ticket

Live Score Preview Prediction Lineups Head 2 Head Live Stream Stats

Saudi Arabia vs Uruguay lineups are yet to be confirmed for Group H Match 15 at the FIFA World Cup 2026. The Saudi Arabia national football team and Uruguay national football team meet on June 15, 2026, at Miami Stadium, with kickoff at 18:00 ET. This projected tracker covers the starting XI, formations, bench options, managers, and matchday lineup changes. This Saudi Arabia national football team vs Uruguay national football team lineups tracker stays projected until official match sheets arrive.

Saudi Arabia have announced a 30-player World Cup group under Georgios Donis, with Salem Al Dawsari, Saud Abdulhamid, Mohammed Kanno, Feras Al Buraikan, and Saleh Al Shehri among the main names. news_items.title: Uruguay loses Arrascaeta, Ronald Araújo and José María Giménez for its 2026 World Cup debut
news_items.author: Thiago Lemos
news_items.text:
Uruguay loses Arrascaeta, Ronald Araújo and José María Giménez for its 2026 World Cup debut

## Mix Vale

Arrascaeta - X.com/ Uruguai

Follow Mix Vale on GoogleGet world news featured in Google SearchFollow

The Uruguay team will have three major absences confirmed for their debut match in the 2026 World Cup against Saudi Arabia. Midfielder Arrascaeta and defenders Ronald Araújo and José María Giménez will not take the field in the clash scheduled for next Monday, at 7pm (Brasília time), in Miami, in the United States.

The seriousness of Arrascaeta’s physical situation became evident this Thursday, when the player was seen limping heavily when disembarking the delegation bus. The Flamengo athlete is treating a grade 2 calf injury, a problem that generated public friction between the Rio club and the Uruguayan association.

Bueno, dale que estamos 🇲🇽 pic.twitter.com/oqpGEszzhu

> — Selección Uruguaya (@Uruguay) June 10, 2026

## Medical crisis generates friction between Flamengo and Uruguay

Arrascaeta’s injury occurred during training with the national team. The medical impasse caused Flamengo to send physiotherapist Laniyan Neves to Cancun, Mexico, with the aim of closely monit

# Team News: Saudi Arabia vs. Uruguay injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Matt Law| 14 Jun 2026 23:00, Last updated: 14 Jun 2026 23:00

© Iconsport / DeFodi Images

Saudi Arabia and Uruguay will begin their respective 2026 World Cup campaigns with a contest in Miami on Monday.

The two nations are competing in Group H this summer alongside Spain and Cape Verde, and here Sports Mole rounds up the team news for the game.

---

## SAUDI ARABIA VS. URUGUAY

## SAUDI ARABIA

Out: Nawaf Al Aqidi (muscle)

Doubtful:

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Al-Owais; Abdulhamid, Al Tambakti, Lajami, Bu; Kanno, Al-Khaibari; N Al-Dawsari, Al-Juwayr, S Al-Dawsari; Al-Buraikan

## URUGUAY

Out: None

Doubtful: Jose Gimenez(ankle), Ronald Araujo(calf), Giorgian de Arrascaeta (calf), Matias Vina(muscle), Sebastian Caceres (head)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Muslera; Varela, Ca"}

# Saudi Arabia vs. Uruguay projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group H game in Miami Gardens

Joe Wright

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Saudi Arabia face Uruguay on Monday as Group H play at the 2026 World Cup begins.

Spain take on Cape Verde in the earlier game before Marcelo Bielsa's team take on a Saudi side they met at this stage of the competition back in 2018.

The European champions are expected to finish top of this pool, but Uruguay will believe they can challenge La Roja and will be eager to lay down an early marker. For Georgios Donis and his players, this game is all about finding a way to get a result that will give them at least some hope of progressing to the Round of 32.

Below is the latest team news and projected lineups for both sides.

2026 WORLD CUP HQ: Latest World Cup news| Full World Cup schedule| Buy World Cup ticket"}

The Uruguay team will have three major absences confirmed for their debut match in the 2026 World Cup against Saudi Arabia. Midfielder Arrascaeta and defenders Ronald Araújo and José María Giménez will not take the field in the clash scheduled for next Monday, at 7pm (Brasília time), in Miami, in the United States.

The seriousness of Arrascaeta’s physical situation became evident this Thursday, when the player was seen limping heavily when disembarking the delegation bus. The Flamengo athlete is treating a grade 2 calf injury, a problem that generated public friction between the Rio club and the Uruguayan association.
